rankingCriteria: likely semantic effect on downstream token choice or bounded class decision
novelty value once promoted into runtime governance

operatorFamilies.captureHook: capture the pre-softmax score window before normalization and compare bounded post-softmax probabilities after the real dispatch
operatorFamilies.rerunShape: single normalization rerun with bounded probability comparison plus optional one-step downstream check when the semantic boundary is close
reasons: Softmax denominator is a classic numeric cliff with a direct semantic story buyers can understand.
It expands Doe beyond norm and projection operators without requiring full-model replay.

operatorFamilies.captureHook: reuse the rmsnorm-style ordinary-execution capture path with additional beta capture for the bounded normalization window
operatorFamilies.rerunShape: single-operator rerun over the bounded normalization window with stable serial and optional cpu-f64 reference variants
reasons: Layernorm shares the most infrastructure with live rmsnorm support, so it is the fastest next implementation after softmax.
It broadens ordinary-execution coverage without inventing a new cost model.

operatorFamilies.captureHook: capture the bounded input/output window around the executed MLP dispatch and attach a downstream semantic check when the output feeds a watched head
operatorFamilies.rerunShape: single-operator rerun with bounded downstream comparison, escalating to a one-step suffix check only when the local output disagreement persists
reasons: MLP drift can materially change later choice without being dismissed as a last-layer-only trick.
Landing it would strengthen Doe's claim that numeric governance spans the wider decode path.

operatorFamilies.captureHook: capture both residual branches at the merge point and compare the bounded merged output immediately after dispatch
operatorFamilies.rerunShape: single-operator rerun over the merge window with cheap bounded output comparison
reasons: Residual adds are cheap to watch and can reveal compounded drift after multiple branches rejoin.
They are a good low-cost expansion once the higher-value normalization and softmax steps are in place.

operatorFamilies.captureHook: reuse the LM-head bounded-slice capture path for classifier, scorer, or reranker heads attached to local workflows
operatorFamilies.rerunShape: single head-score rerun with bounded class comparison and no suffix replay by default
reasons: Task heads map directly onto buyer-facing approve/deny, redact/release, and allow/block workflows.
They are the easiest way to extend numeric governance beyond next-token generation into classifier-style products.
